title: [lora_l2.c:479] lichen_lora_l2_deinit state inconsistency window
description: Function: lichen_lora_l2_deinit. Contract violation: Between lines 540 (atomic_set initialized=0) and 612 (atomic_set deiniting=0), the module is in an inconsistent state: initialized is false but cleanup is ongoing. A racing init() call would pass the deiniting check (line 490) but fail at line 298 (already initialized check). The atomic_cas at line 490 prevents concurrent deinit, but the state machine has a brief window where initialized=0 but deiniting=1 could confuse callers checking lichen_lora_l2_is_running() or get_eui64(). Priority 3 - edge case with correct behavior but subtle semantics.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed final DEINITING->UNINIT transition to use lora_transition_from() (atomic CAS) instead of lora_transition() to eliminate the state inconsistency window. The non-atomic read-then-write pattern could theoretically allow state corruption between the atomic_get() and atomic_set() calls.
